claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you live in the UK and have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children, I need to gather some details about their circumstances.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

**How old is Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you.

**How old is Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie is 10 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (10 years old).

Now I need to confirm your living arrangements with your children.

**Do you live with all three of your children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live with all of them. Alex and Charlie live with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Alex and Charlie live with you, but Blake lives with someone else.

**Can you tell me - who does Blake live with, and how long has Blake been living with them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent. They've been living there for quite a while now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about your situation with Blake.

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep - such as payments for food, clothes, pocket money, or housing - either through a court order, agreement, or informal arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now, let me check on your circumstances as the claimant.

**Do you have any of the following: settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen or have another right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

**Are Alex and Charlie currently in hospital, in care, or in residential accommodation paid for by your local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex and Charlie are both at home with me. They're not in hospital or in care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Thank you for that information.

I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. Let me review your circumstances:

- **Alex (3 years old)**: Lives with you at home in the UK
- **Blake (8 years old)**: Lives with their other parent, and you are not making financial contributions towards their upkeep
- **Charlie (10 years old)**: Lives with you at home in the UK

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Eligibility

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.**

### Breakdown:

- **✓ Alex (3 years old)**: **ELIGIBLE** - Alex lives with you in the UK and is under 16 years old, so you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

- **✗ Blake (8 years old)**: **NOT ELIGIBLE** - Blake has been living with their other parent for an extended period beyond the initial 8-week grace period. Since you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep, you are no longer entitled to claim Child Benefit for Blake. The other parent is likely claiming for Blake instead.

- **✓ Charlie (10 years old)**: **ELIGIBLE** - Charlie lives with you in the UK and is under 16 years old, so you can claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you start making regular financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep - you may want to contact HMRC to discuss this further.

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as you live with them. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16 years old, living with you, and you live in the UK. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old but lives with their other parent. According to the rules, you would normally only get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after Blake goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims. Since Blake has been living with their other parent for quite a while (beyond the 8-week period), and you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep, you are no longer eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. The other parent may be claiming Child Benefit for Blake instead.

Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie as you live with them. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16 years old, living with you, and you live in the UK. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are a British citizen living in the UK with three children. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, as both children live with you and are under 16 years old. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, as Blake has been living with their other parent for an extended period beyond the initial 8-week grace period, and you are not making financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, so the other parent is likely claiming for Blake. You should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as you live with them. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16 years old, living with you, and you live in the UK. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old but lives with their other parent. According to the rules, you would normally only get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after Blake goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims. Since Blake has been living with their other parent for quite a while (beyond the 8-week period), and you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep, you are no longer eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. The other parent may be claiming Child Benefit for Blake instead.

Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie as you live with them. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16 years old, living with you, and you live in the UK. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: You are a British citizen living in the UK with three children. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, as both children live with you and are under 16 years old. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, as Blake has been living with their other parent for an extended period beyond the initial 8-week grace period, and you are not making financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, so the other parent is likely claiming for Blake. You should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie.
